behaviors.description: Validate required patient fields before downstream steps and return one error listing all missing required fields.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: Behavior is pure validation/output formatting for one input object, with no module handoff required to trigger the failure. Lower-level miss-proof: Unit is sufficient; Integration/System/Agentic are disqualified because behavior text has no OS/network/subprocess/AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Call the validator with payloads missing different combinations of `name`, `date_of_birth`, and `insurance_id`; assert downstream gate is false and the error contains exactly all missing field names in one response.

behaviors.description: Reject invalid DOB values (future, older than 150 years, non-date strings).
behaviors.justification: Evidence: This is deterministic date parsing/range logic in a single validation boundary. Lower-level miss-proof: Unit catches real failures directly; higher levels are disqualified because no cross-module or real infra behavior is required by the spec text.
behaviors.test_description: Test DOB validator with boundary and malformed inputs (`today+1`, `today-151y`, `"not-a-date"`) and valid controls; assert specific rejection reasons.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility request uses correct payer payload fields and correctly maps `eligible`/`not eligible`/`pending` responses.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: Behavior explicitly targets an external payer API boundary and status interpretation at real HTTP/API contract level. Lower-level miss-proof: Integration with in-process mocks can hide wire-format/header/path/query issues; real HTTP interaction is needed to catch contract failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real HTTP payer sandbox/test server, submit known patient/plan fixtures, capture outbound request fields (`patient_id`, `plan_code`) and assert response mapping for the three statuses.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, mark eligibility `pending` and continue intake flow.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: Failure mode is transport unreachability (`API is unreachable`), which is an infra/network condition. Lower-level miss-proof: Mocked exceptions often miss real timeout/refused/DNS classes and retry timing behavior; real unreachable HTTP conditions are the failure boundary.
behaviors.test_description: Execute intake with payer endpoint intentionally unreachable (e.g., blackholed host or refused port) and assert eligibility becomes `pending` and pipeline proceeds to triage/scheduling.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation payload contains reported symptoms plus configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: This checks module handoff from intake/config assembly into the model client payload. Lower-level miss-proof: Unit on payload builder alone may miss wiring/config injection bugs; higher levels are disqualified because behavior text targets payload construction, not model output quality.
behaviors.test_description: Run service-layer triage call with stubbed model endpoint; assert outbound request body includes exact symptoms list and active rubric content from configuration.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage marks emergency symptom combinations (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath) as high urgency.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: Correctness depends on real model judgment quality for clinical urgency classification. Lower-level miss-proof: Any stubbed model response can trivially pass and cannot detect misclassification by the real AI; System-only infra checks are insufficient without real model behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency symptom fixtures and assert high-urgency classification rate/threshold meets safety expectation.

behaviors.description: Triage result parsing/persistence stores a clinician-readable explanation field, not just numeric urgency.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: Behavior is about handoff from triage result parsing into storage schema/record mapping. Lower-level miss-proof: Unit parse tests can miss dropped field at repository/service boundary; higher levels are disqualified because explanation quality itself is not evaluated here.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage response containing score + explanation through service to DB; assert persisted intake record includes non-empty explanation column/text field.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation is case-specific (mentions symptoms and rationale), not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: This is output-quality evaluation of generated rationale content from the live model. Lower-level miss-proof: Deterministic fixtures cannot verify whether the model actually produces specific, symptom-grounded explanations under real inference variability.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on multiple symptom cases; assert explanation text references case symptoms and includes urgency reasoning signals, failing generic/template responses.

behaviors.description: Scheduler selects first available slot satisfying urgency SLA (high same-day, medium within 48h).
behaviors.justification: Evidence: Failure appears at scheduling logic + calendar DB query/update boundary across modules. Lower-level miss-proof: Unit-only policy tests can miss query ordering/availability interaction; higher levels are disqualified because no real external network/AI dependency is required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Use local test DB with seeded provider availability; run scheduler for high/medium urgency and assert chosen slot is earliest one within the required time window.

behaviors.description: Concurrent intake processing must not double-book the same slot.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: Real failure is concurrency/transaction interaction in scheduling modules against shared calendar DB. Lower-level miss-proof: Unit tests cannot reproduce locking/race behavior; System is not required because a local real DB transaction test can expose the race without external infra.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling transactions targeting same candidate slot on local DB with production isolation/constraints; assert only one booking succeeds and the other is re-routed or fails safely.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message contains required fields and visit-type-specific preparation instructions.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: Behavior depends on composing data from patient + appointment + provider + visit-type instruction sources. Lower-level miss-proof: Unit template tests may miss missing-field propagation across service boundaries; higher levels are disqualified because no real OS/network/AI dependence is stated.
behaviors.test_description: Execute confirmation generation from persisted appointment context and visit type mapping; assert final message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and matching prep instructions.

behaviors.description: Orchestration pipeline (validate→insurance→triage→schedule→confirm) completes with deterministic stubbed triage.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: This explicitly targets cross-module orchestration with deterministic stubs, i.e., wiring/state handoff not model judgment. Lower-level miss-proof: Unit tests cannot catch stage sequencing/state propagation bugs; Agentic is disqualified by behavior text `deterministic stubbed triage output`.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end intake in-process with stub triage and controlled insurance/calendar dependencies; assert each stage executes in order and final confirmation is emitted with expected state transitions.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Evidence: Pipeline includes live AI triage call; completion robustness depends on real model output shape/content in addition to real infra. Lower-level miss-proof: System-only tests with mocked AI can miss failures caused by actual model responses; Workflow is disqualified because behavior describes one agentic decision point, not multiple agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like intake against live payer API and live triage model for a real submission fixture; assert autonomous completion through confirmation with no operator intervention.
